# Edify OS — Archetype Integrations Roadmap

**Generated by:** Lopmon (CLM Studios)
**Date:** 2026-04-13
**Approved by:** Z (pending review)

---

## The Vision

Each of the 6 AI directors connects to real services — pulling grants, sending emails, managing calendars, posting to social media. Not a chatbot wearing a name tag. A real team.

## The Phases

Each phase ships independently. The org gets smarter directors after every phase.

---

### Phase 1: Foundation + Smart Directors
**What:** Set up the backend, upgrade all 6 director prompts with skill instructions, clean up Finance Director.

**Work involved:**
- Set up Supabase project (database, auth, file storage)
- Create .env.local with real Supabase keys
- Switch from static export to server mode (so API routes work)
- Wire existing API routes to real database (conversations, org data, memory)
- Rewrite 6 archetype prompts with personality + skills from ARCHETYPE_DESIGNS.md
- Update chat UI with skill-specific suggested prompts
- Remove Finance Director from everywhere

**What the user gets:** Directors that produce real deliverables (grant lists, meeting agendas, content calendars, etc.) through smart prompting. Conversations and org data persist in a real database instead of disappearing when you clear your browser.

**Cost:** $0 (Supabase free tier)

---

### Phase 2: Google Workspace
**What:** Connect Google Calendar, Gmail, and Google Drive. This is the biggest value unlock because it touches multiple directors.

**Integrations:**
- Google Calendar API — read/write events, check availability, scheduling
- Gmail API — read inbox, draft/send emails, search
- Google Drive API — read/create documents, file storage

**Which directors benefit:**
- Executive Assistant: Read calendar, prep for meetings, draft emails, track action items from real inbox
- Events Director: Create event timelines as calendar entries, send invitations
- Marketing Director: Draft newsletter emails, schedule sends
- Development Director: Draft and send donor outreach emails

**What the user gets:** "Prep me for tomorrow's board meeting" and the EA actually pulls your real calendar, finds the meeting, checks who's attending, and drafts an agenda. "Send a thank-you to our $5K donor" and the Development Director actually drafts and sends the email through Gmail.

**Cost:** $0 (Google APIs are free)

**Technical:** OAuth 2.0 flow — user authorizes Edify to access their Google account. Tokens stored encrypted in Supabase. The integration code Milo already scaffolded covers most of this.

---

### Phase 3: Grants & Fundraising
**What:** Connect to grant databases and build a donor CRM in Supabase.

**Integrations:**
- Grants.gov API — search federal grants by keyword, eligibility, deadline (FREE)
- SAM.gov API — federal contracts, grant opportunities, entity registration data (FREE)
- Built-in CRM — donor records, giving history, engagement tracking (all in Supabase)

**Which directors benefit:**
- Development Director: Search real grant databases, track applications, manage donor relationships
- Programs Director: Pull grant requirements for compliance reporting

**What the user gets:** "Find grants for youth mentoring programs over $10K" and the Development Director searches real databases, returns actual grants with real deadlines and real dollar amounts. Donor CRM tracks every interaction so "draft a re-engagement letter for lapsed donors" pulls real giving history.

**Cost:** $0 (Grants.gov is free, CRM is built in Supabase)

---

### Phase 4: Social Media & Marketing
**What:** Connect social media platforms for content publishing and analytics.

**Integrations:**
- Facebook/Instagram Graph API — post content, read engagement metrics
- LinkedIn API — publish posts, company page management
- X/Twitter API — post tweets, read mentions (free tier)
- SendGrid — send email campaigns, newsletters (free: 100/day)

**Which directors benefit:**
- Marketing Director: Post directly to social platforms, track what's performing, send newsletters
- Development Director: Share campaign updates, donor appreciation posts

**What the user gets:** "Post this to LinkedIn and Instagram" and it actually posts. "What's our best performing content this month?" pulls real analytics. "Send this month's newsletter to our email list" actually sends it.

**Cost:** $0 (all free tiers, SendGrid free for small lists)

---

### Phase 5: Programs & People
**What:** Build participant tracking, volunteer management, and HR document systems in Supabase.

**Integrations:**
- Participant tracking system (built in Supabase — intake forms, attendance, outcomes)
- Survey/forms engine (built in-app or Google Forms API)
- Document generation (produce PDFs for policies, reports, job descriptions)
- Eventbrite API — event registration, ticketing

**Which directors benefit:**
- Programs Director: Track participants, record outcomes, generate funder reports from real data
- HR & Volunteer Coordinator: Manage volunteer database, track hours, produce HR documents
- Events Director: Create events on Eventbrite, track registrations, manage sponsors

**What the user gets:** "How many participants completed the program this quarter?" pulls real data. "Generate our Q2 outcome report for the Morrison Foundation" compiles actual numbers into a funder-ready narrative. "Create a volunteer signup for Saturday's event" sets it up on Eventbrite.

**Cost:** $0 (Supabase + Eventbrite free tiers)

---

## Phase Summary

| Phase | What Ships | Directors Upgraded | Cost | Complexity |
|-------|-----------|-------------------|------|------------|
| 1. Foundation | Smart prompts + real database | All 6 | $0 | Medium |
| 2. Google | Calendar, email, docs | EA, Events, Marketing, Dev | $0 | Medium |
| 3. Grants & CRM | Grant search + donor tracking | Dev, Programs | $0 | Medium-High |
| 4. Social & Email | Post + send + analytics | Marketing, Dev | $0 | Medium |
| 5. Programs & People | Participant tracking + events | Programs, HR, Events | $0 | High |

## Total Infrastructure Cost: $0/month

- Supabase free tier: 500MB database, auth, file storage
- Vercel free tier: frontend hosting + serverless functions
- Google APIs: free
- Social APIs: free tiers
- Grants.gov: free
- SendGrid: free (100 emails/day)
- Eventbrite: free for free events
- Claude API: BYOK (users bring their own key)

The only cost to the end user is their Claude API key (~$1-3/day in typical usage, as Milo noted on the pricing page).

---

## Recommended Order

Start with **Phase 1** (foundation + smart prompts) because everything else depends on having a real database. Then **Phase 2** (Google) because it's free, touches the most directors, and has the highest "wow factor" — an EA that reads your real calendar is the kind of thing that sells the product.

Phases 3-5 can be done in any order depending on what Z's early users need most.
